div.head: Memorandum by the Assistant Secretary of State for Inter-American Affairs (Miller) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Some time ago you suggested the preparation of an article on inter-American policy similar to the “X” article.Documents in file 611.20 indicate that a draft of this article existed at least as early as February 1950. Such an article was prepared by Louis Halle, Planning Adviser in ARA, and was published in the July issue of Foreign Affairs.“Y,” “On a Certain Impatience with Latin America,” Foreign Affairs, vol. 28, no. 4, (July 1950), pp. 565–579. A copy of the magazine is attached and I hope that you will have time to read this excellent restatement of our Latin American policy.

This policy line has been reflected in three speeches which I have made this year, at the Rotary Club in Charleston, West Virginia last January,Press Release No. 27, which contains Mr. Miller’s speech of January 11, is included in Department of State Press Releases, 1950, under date. at the Philadelphia Bulletin Forum on March 22“Inter-American Relations in Perspective,” Department of State Bulletin, April 3, 1950, p. 521. and at the meeting of the Pennsylvania Federation of Labor May 9.”The American Way and Standards of Democracy,” ibid., May 22, 1950, p. 797.
